programme.title: Quo Vadis TV
programme.desc: Quo Vadis TV is committed to sharing the Biblical message in a way that makes Christian living practical, realistic, and attainable. Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

programme.title: 1997 Forever
programme.desc: A retro-futuristic snowboarding odyssey.

programme.title: Spinning at the Speed of Now
programme.desc: The spotlight is on Filipe Toledo as he travels with Josh Kerr, Damien Hobgood, Carlos Muñoz, Miguel Pupo, Stevie Pittman and Victor Bernardo.

programme.title: Drive Thru South Central America
programme.desc: Rio, Brazil The crew goes to Rio, Brazil for some sun, surf and hang gliding.

programme.title: Camp Woodward
programme.desc: S08 E13 Berronte heads back to the orthopedist to check in on his wrist; Gary, Damonte, and Johnny Jones check out the powerslide contest; the whole crew heads to the Mini Mega to learn how to fly.

programme.title: The Adventures of Danny & the Dingo
programme.desc: S03 E06 The Grenade Games The Grenade crew heads to Whistler, B.C., for the 5th annual Grenade Games.

programme.title: Built to Shred
programme.desc: Surf Rail of Death Jeff King and crew take on the world's first surf rail.

programme.title: Thrillbillies : Do or Die
programme.desc: The lifestyle of Tenacious J; Travis Pastrana backflips a motorcycle over the Grand Canyon.

programme.title: Firsthand : Lisa Andersen
programme.desc: Following one of the top female surfers around, Lisa Andersen.

programme.title: Drive Thru South Central America
programme.desc: Search for Waves The crew searches for new waves after getting beat up at the beach break.

programme.title: Behind the Gate
programme.desc: Hail to the Kings Febvre fights for points, Herlings rules Kegums, MX2 heats up, chaos and glory in the Latvian sand!

programme.title: Bubba's World : The Trip
programme.desc: James is sidelined by a shoulder injury.

programme.title: M80 : Scott Stephens
programme.desc: Check out featured rider Scott Stephens.

programme.title: The Gypsy Tour
programme.desc: The Cliché Skateboards team set off as gypsies, finding any and every way to get by with virtually zero cash, as well as getting in some great skating.

programme.title: Camp Woodward
programme.desc: Beijing The top stars in action sports travel to Beijing, China to help introduce the lifestyle of action sports to the Chinese youth.

programme.title: FireRide Festival: The Beginning
programme.desc: The FireRide Festival in Puerto Vallarta, Mexico, is a two-day bike event where a field of riders dispute the prizes in the High Jump Challenge, Best Trick and Whip-off Battle; with Sam Pilgrim, Nicholi Rogatkin, Cody Johnson and Casey Brown.

programme.title: The Moto: Inside the Outdoors
programme.desc: The Dream Ryan Dungey has won five championships, sacrificing everything for his dream. He is the personification of modern motocross.

programme.title: Built to Shred
programme.desc: Camp Shredleton Jeff King and his crew shred a storage yard of decommissioned tanks.

programme.title: Thrillbillies : Tom Spond
programme.desc: Dusty has a few secrets up his sleeve that come in handy for a covert operation.

programme.title: The Adventures of Danny & the Dingo
programme.desc: S04 E10 Dachstein The Dingo pulls a frontside 720 melon in Dachstein.

programme.title: Standard Snowboard Show : Alaska and BC Backcountry
programme.desc: Exploration of the BC Backcountry; road trip to Alaska.

programme.title: Firsthand : Rob Machado
programme.desc: Following surf champion Rob Machado as he travels to Japan.

programme.title: 1997 Forever
programme.desc: A retro-futuristic snowboarding odyssey.

programme.title: Livin' Tiny
programme.desc: Friends convert a moving trailer into a home for five and go on a Canadian ski trip adventure; they face extreme adventures and document their experiences.

programme.title: Fuel TV presents: Mission Caribbean
programme.desc: Three of the world's best wakeboarders and four surfers set out for an eight-day surf and wake exploration.

programme.title: On Surfari
programme.desc: Panamá Shayne and Shannon McIntyre's love of surfing and obscure locations brings them more than waves.

programme.title: Drive Thru Europe : Halloween in France
programme.desc: The crew gets ready to go home.

programme.title: Behind the Gate
programme.desc: One Last Dance In-depth analysis of the Oriental Beauty Valley MXGP of China, a turning point for both World Championships with Jorge Prado taking the red plate off the bike of Tim Gajser.

programme.title: Bubba's World : The Sponsors
programme.desc: Still unable to ride, James answers his sponsors' call and jets out to California.

programme.title: Camp Woodward
programme.desc: S07 E09 Action-sports counselors and guest professionals train skateboarding and BMX students.

programme.title: Check 1, 2
programme.desc: Duane Peters & the Hunns Featuring legendary punk rock icon and skateboarder Duane Peters.

programme.title: Standard Snowboard Show : Hemsedal
programme.desc: Burton Hemsedal sessions in Norway.

programme.title: The Captain and Casey Show
programme.desc: Jeron Wilson The Captain and Casey Show

programme.title: Firsthand : Kevin Pearce
programme.desc: Arctic Challenge winner Kevin Pearce competes in Vermont; he sets off on a tour across California, Utah and Colorado.

programme.title: Way of the Ocean: Australia
programme.desc: The connection between man and sea through poetic motion.

programme.title: Arc of Aleutia
programme.desc: Three surfers retrace the brutal path of a legend in the turbulent and isolated Aleutian Islands of Alaska.

programme.title: The Great Ride Open : Ocotillo
programme.desc: The caravan continues touring the Imperial Valley and heads towards Ocotillo Wells.

programme.title: Built to Shred
programme.desc: Snow Ranch Jeff King and the team blow fresh snow to create a winter wonderland at the Ranch.

programme.title: The Adventures of Danny & the Dingo
programme.desc: S03 E05 US Open The team leaves Spain and heads to Stratton Mountain, Vt.

programme.title: Standard Snowboard Show : Torstein Horgmo
programme.desc: Snowboarder Torstein Horgmo.

programme.title: The Captain and Casey Show
programme.desc: President The Captain and Casey Show

programme.title: M80 : Jim McNeil
programme.desc: Featured rider Jim McNeil; the "Hill Kids"; a contest at the Quarry.

programme.title: The Moto: Inside the Outdoors
programme.desc: Sport of Attrition Taking a look at various grueling training regimens.

programme.title: Way of the Ocean: Australia
programme.desc: The connection between man and sea through poetic motion.

programme.title: Vans Get-N Classic
programme.desc: Following John Florence, Joel Tudor, Nathan Fletcher, the Gudauskas brothers and many others in a search for every surfer's dream, to find and surf the perfect wave.

programme.title: Drive Thru South Central America
programme.desc: Arrival in Chile The surf crew arrives in Chile.

programme.title: Camp Woodward
programme.desc: S08 E14 Episode 14 Action-sports counselors and guest professionals train skateboarding and BMX students.

programme.title: The Adventures of Danny & the Dingo
programme.desc: S04 E05 Trophy Truck Rally Danny and The Dingo take a trip to Odessa, TX to compete in a trophy truck race.

programme.title: Built to Shred
programme.desc: Solar Shred Manufacturing eco-friendly boards.

programme.title: Thrillbillies : Wet & Wild
programme.desc: Cam and Crum learn to wheelie street bikes and do some jet ski motocross.

programme.title: Firsthand : Ronnie Renner
programme.desc: Ronnie Renner rides in Palmdale before heading to Budapest.

programme.title: Drive Thru South Central America
programme.desc: Leaving Panama The crew barely makes it to the beach after letting Donovan drive.

programme.title: Behind the Gate
programme.desc: Hope and Glory Triumph's MX rise and UK motocross heritage shine at the thrilling Matterley Basin GP.

programme.title: Bubba's World : The Tourist
programme.desc: James Stewart tries to recapture childhood moments.

programme.title: M80 : Loose Ends 2
programme.desc: Clifford Adaptante, Matt Rebeaud Blowin' Up, a road trip to Beilson's Compound.

programme.title: Riding the Long White Cloud
programme.desc: Pro skateboarders tour New Zealand.

programme.title: YT MOB : Homelife
programme.desc: S01 E03 At home with the three riders of The YT Mob 2020: Oisin O'Callaghan, Guy Johnston and David Trummer; a look at their home trails and how they train for the upcoming races.

programme.title: The Moto: Inside the Outdoors
programme.desc: The Battle Begins Riders prepare for the grueling season ahead.

programme.title: Built to Shred
programme.desc: Storming Kona Jeff King and crew visit KONA skatepark in Florida.

programme.title: Thrillbillies : Clay's Dream
programme.desc: Clay has a crazy dream that stays with him.

programme.title: The Adventures of Danny & the Dingo
programme.desc: S04 E11 Italy to Switzerland Big jumps on a glacier in Italy; Dingo milks a goat.

programme.title: Spot to Spot
programme.desc: S01 E03 Jake Illardi Jake Ilardi returns to Sarasota, revisiting the spots that shaped his style.

programme.title: Harvey Spannos
programme.desc: A young boy uncovers the mystery of his father's death and searches for his own identity.

programme.title: Side by Side
programme.desc: The most influential and diverse line up of athletes.

programme.title: The Line Between Good and Evil
programme.desc: The North Face team captain Hilaree Nelson's 20-year quest to ski India's Peak of Good and Evil, Papsura.

programme.title: On Surfari
programme.desc: Island of Mauritius Shayne and Shannon McIntyre's love of surfing and obscure locations brings them more than waves.

programme.title: Drive Thru South Africa : The Beginning
programme.desc: The crew arrives in Cape Town to start their two-week adventure.

programme.title: Behind the Gate
programme.desc: Monster Energy FIM Motocross of Nations The 77th edition of the motocross event of the season: the Monster Energy FIM Motocross of Nations 2024 at Matterley Basin; riders from all around the world take part in this race, eight world champions and seven American champions.

programme.title: Bubba's World : The Doctor
programme.desc: The racing season is nearly half over and James Stewart is still unable to race.

programme.title: Camp Woodward
programme.desc: S07 E10 Action-sports counselors and guest professionals train skateboarding and BMX students.

programme.title: Check 1, 2
programme.desc: Mike V. and the Rats The musical life of professional skater Mike V.

programme.desc: The early days of freestyle snowboarding in Lake Tahoe.

programme.title: Sands of Iwo Jima
programme.desc: A Marine sergeant (John Wayne) pushes a colonel's son (John Agar) and other recruits until they're ready to fight.

programme.title: The Caine Mutiny
programme.desc: A World War II Naval officer is court-martialed for relieving paranoid Capt. Queeg of command during a typhoon.

programme.title: Attack!
programme.desc: A cowardly captain (Eddie Albert) dooms a Battle of the Bulge platoon, but one man (Jack Palance) makes it back.

programme.title: Cattle King
programme.desc: President Arthur helps a rich rancher (Robert Taylor) fight advocates of open grazing in the Territory of Wyoming.

programme.title: The Nevadan
programme.desc: An undercover U.S. marshal (Randolph Scott) and a crooked rancher follow an outlaw (Forrest Tucker) to stolen gold.

programme.title: Sands of Iwo Jima
programme.desc: Marine Sgt. John Stryker (John Wayne) is despised by his own men for his rough attitude and exhausting training regimen. As the war in the Pacific progresses, though, they begin to respect Stryker's hard-edged outlook on war and his brutal training methods, as it has helped them prepare for the harsh realities of the battlefield. They'll need all of Stryker's battle tactics if they want to survive what will end up being one of the bloodiest engagements of the war: the Battle of Iwo Jima.

programme.title: Destry Rides Again
programme.desc: The small town of Bottleneck is under the control of Kent (Brian Donlevy), a power-hungry boss who gets control over the local cattle ranchers by winning a rigged game of cards. When the local sheriff questions the legitimacy of the game, Kent has him killed and names the town drunk, Dimsdale (Charles Winninger), as sheriff. What Kent doesn't know is that Dimsdale knows legendary lawman Tom Destry, who in turns sends his daring son Tom Destry Jr. (James Stewart) to Bottleneck to save the day.

programme.title: A Month in the Country
programme.desc: Two shaken soldiers (Colin Firth, Kenneth Branagh) recover from World War I one summer in a Yorkshire village.

programme.title: Laramie Mountains
programme.desc: The Durango Kid learns that crooked scouts are behind attempts to escalate a war between a local tribe and the cavalry.

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A murder investigation leads Holmes and Watson on a wild goose chase.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Allen Pinkerton, hoping to recover stolen money, interferes in Earp's investigation of a murder in Wichita and gets the judge to pull Earp's badge.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Battle lines are drawn and everyone comes out swinging when a women's suffrage movement hits town.

programme.title: Kelly's Heroes
programme.desc: In the midst of World War II, an array of colorful American soldiers gets inside information from a drunk German officer about millions of dollars worth of gold hidden on enemy soil. Kelly (Clint Eastwood), a private with the platoon, devises a plan to sneak past the German officers to steal the loot for his crew. They recruit more men and set their plan into action. Despite several casualties, the men are determined to press forward, even if it means striking a deal with the opposing army.

programme.title: Paid Programming: Carshield -- Cars Have Changed
Destry Rides Again
programme.desc: The small town of Bottleneck is under the control of Kent (Brian Donlevy), a power-hungry boss who gets control over the local cattle ranchers by winning a rigged game of cards. When the local sheriff questions the legitimacy of the game, Kent has him killed and names the town drunk, Dimsdale (Charles Winninger), as sheriff. What Kent doesn't know is that Dimsdale knows legendary lawman Tom Destry, who in turns sends his daring son Tom Destry Jr. (James Stewart) to Bottleneck to save the day.

programme.title: The Nevadan
programme.desc: On his way to jail, bandit Tom Tanner (Forrest Tucker) escapes the law and is pursued by Andy Barclay (Randolph Scott), a federal marshal who believes that Tanner will lead him to a stash of stolen gold. Barclay changes horses at the ranch of Karen Galt (Dorothy Malone), planting the seeds of romance before following Tanner to a mine shaft where the stashed loot is hidden. But a gang of outlaws who also want the gold then arrives, forcing Tanner and Barclay to team up and fend them off together.

programme.title: The Hanging Tree
programme.desc: Joseph "Doc" Frail (Gary Cooper) is a doctor with a past he's trying to outrun. While in Montana, he comes across a mining camp with a hanging tree and rescues a man named Rune (Ben Piazza) from the noose. With Rune as his servant, Frail decides to settle down, and he takes over as town doctor. He meets Elizabeth (Maria Schell), who is suffering from shock, and the two soon fall in love. But when Elizabeth is attacked, Frail's attempt to help her land them both in trouble.

programme.title: Riding Shotgun
programme.desc: A cowboy is hired to ride shotgun on a stagecoach, but his personal mission is to find a brutal killer and deliver justice. After the cowboy is lured from his post by a clue that might lead to the killer, he leaves the stagecoach unprotected.

programme.title: The Quick Gun
programme.desc: A banished gunfighter (Audie Murphy) returns to his sweetheart (Merry Anders) and helps his sheriff friend (James Best) face outlaws.

programme.title: Kelly's Heroes
programme.desc: In the midst of World War II, an array of colorful American soldiers gets inside information from a drunk German officer about millions of dollars worth of gold hidden on enemy soil. Kelly (Clint Eastwood), a private with the platoon, devises a plan to sneak past the German officers to steal the loot for his crew. They recruit more men and set their plan into action. Despite several casualties, the men are determined to press forward, even if it means striking a deal with the opposing army.

programme.title: Hell Is for Heroes
programme.desc: During World War II, a small group of soldiers led by the brave Sergeant Larkin (Harry Guardino) anticipate returning home after fighting on the Siegfried Line in France. Among them are Reese (Steve McQueen), a rebellious loner who lives for battle, and Corby (Bobby Darin), a slick hustler. However, the squad is then informed that they must go back to the front lines, where they are vastly outnumbered and forced to fight within the reach of a menacing German pillbox.

programme.title: Breaker Morant
programme.desc: In South Africa during the Second Boer War, Australian Army Lieutenants Harry "Breaker" Morant (Edward Woodward), Peter Handcock (Bryan Brown) and George Witton (Lewis Fitz-Gerald) stand accused of the murders of six Boer prisoners and the assassination of a German missionary. British Commander-in-Chief Lord Kitchener is determined to see the men found guilty, which he can use as a bargaining chip in an impending peace conference with the Boers.

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Holmes investigates a death and the disappearance of plans for a secret submarine.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Petticoat Junction
programme.desc: Bobbie Jo falls for Alan Landman (Dennis Hopper), a poet and beatnik.

programme.desc: Discover the Nectar Tri-Comfort Cooling Pillow for the ultimate sleep experience! Adjustable to three firmness levels to provide customized support and cooling all night long. Order NOW to get a second pillow FREE, plus a 30-night home trial!

programme.title: Ministry - Your Words Have Power with Joyce Meyer
programme.desc: Your words hold incredible power; today, Joyce reveals how the things you say can impact your life in profound ways.

programme.title: Word of God Ministries
programme.desc: Pastor James McMenis and his wife Chrissy are dedicated to winning the lost to Jesus and making disciples of the saved. Their goal is to bring clarity to the Word of God, making it life-applicable to people everywhere.

programme.title: The Catholic Mass
programme.desc: The Heart of the nation Sunday mass brings the beauty and traditions of the Catholic faith.

programme.title: Perry Stone
programme.desc: Religious teachings from Perry Stone.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Ranchers are frustrated by the number of horse thefts near Wichita and want to blame a band of gypsies camped on Gus Cassen's property.

programme.title: The Hanging Tree
programme.desc: Joseph "Doc" Frail (Gary Cooper) is a doctor with a past he's trying to outrun. While in Montana, he comes across a mining camp with a hanging tree and rescues a man named Rune (Ben Piazza) from the noose. With Rune as his servant, Frail decides to settle down, and he takes over as town doctor. He meets Elizabeth (Maria Schell), who is suffering from shock, and the two soon fall in love. But when Elizabeth is attacked, Frail's attempt to help her land them both in trouble.

programme.title: Riding Shotgun
programme.desc: A cowboy is hired to ride shotgun on a stagecoach, but his personal mission is to find a brutal killer and deliver justice. After the cowboy is lured from his post by a clue that might lead to the killer, he leaves the stagecoach unprotected.

programme.title: The Quick Gun
programme.desc: A banished gunfighter (Audie Murphy) returns to his sweetheart (Merry Anders) and helps his sheriff friend (James Best) face outlaws.

programme.title: The Battle at Apache Pass
programme.desc: Indian leader Cochise (Jeff Chandler), whose wife (Susan Cabot) is pregnant, sides with a cavalry major (John Lund) against warring Geronimo.

programme.title: The Guns of Navarone
programme.desc: In 1943, a small commando team is sent to destroy huge German guns on the Greek Island of Navarone in order to rescue Allied troops trapped on Kheros. Led by British Major Franklin (Anthony Quayle), the team includes American Mallory (Gregory Peck), Greek resistance fighter Stavros (Anthony Quinn) and reluctant explosives expert Miller. Facing impossible odds, the men battle stormy seas and daunting cliffs. When Franklin is injured, Mallory takes command, and personal enmities spill over.

programme.title: Twelve O'Clock High
programme.desc: In 1942, an American Air Force unit stationed in England is plagued with morale problems until no-nonsense Brigadier General Frank Savage (Gregory Peck) assumes command. His tough leadership is initially resented by not only his pilots but his second-in-command (Hugh Marlowe), a West Point graduate and son of a general. But, with the help of a hotshot flying ace (Robert Patten) and a sympathetic administrator (Dean Jagger), the unit pulls together into a gung-ho fighting crew.

programme.title: The Outlaw Stallion
programme.desc: A boy and his mother are kidnapped by outlaws after the boy's stallion kills the outlaw leader's mount.

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A curse may be the cause of Sir Charles' death.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Daniel Boone
programme.desc: The frontiersman (George O'Brien) leads settlers to Kentucky, where a white renegade (John Carradine) unites tribes against them.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick
programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: A pillar of the community is hailed as a hero when he shoots a thief, until people learn that the man is wanted for being a member of the James gang.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Allen Pinkerton, hoping to recover stolen money, interferes in Earp's investigation of a murder in Wichita and gets the judge to pull Earp's badge.

programme.title: The Guns of Navarone

programme.title: The Outlaw Stallion
programme.desc: A boy and his mother are kidnapped by outlaws after the boy's stallion kills the outlaw leader's mount.

programme.title: The Battle at Apache Pass
programme.desc: Indian leader Cochise (Jeff Chandler), whose wife (Susan Cabot) is pregnant, sides with a cavalry major (John Lund) against warring Geronimo.

programme.title: Masterson of Kansas
programme.desc: In the 1870s, Marshal Wyatt Earp (Bruce Cowling) calls upon Dodge City, Kan., Sheriff Bat Masterson (George Montgomery) to track down rancher Merrick (John Maxwell), who is accused of murder. But, thanks to some proof held by local Native American Chief Yellow Hawk (Jay Silverheels), Masterson realizes Merrick might be the victim of a frame-up. So, with the help of Earp and gunfighter Doc Holliday (James Griffith), Masterson sets out to find those who are really behind the killing.

programme.title: Hellcats of the Navy
programme.desc: Captain Casey Abbott (Ronald Reagan) commands an American submarine in the Pacific during World War II. When a perilous mission to retrieve a new Japanese mine for study goes awry, he's forced to abandon a likable combat diver, Wes Barton (Harry Lauter). Abbott's orders to leave a man behind appear to be justified, but his second in command, Don Landon (Arthur Franz), believes he had ulterior motives: Barton had been competing for the attention of his love interest, Helen (Nancy Davis).

programme.title: The Bridges at Toko-Ri
programme.desc: Naval Reservist pilot Lt. Harry Brubaker (William Holden) is stationed on an aircraft carrier in the Sea of Japan during the Korean War. Although suffering from battle fatigue and longing to return to civilian life with his wife, Nancy (Grace Kelly), he agrees to fly a dangerous bombing mission over North Korea, with the support of his faithful rescue crew, Forney (Mickey Rooney) and Gamidge (Earl Holliman), and his sympathetic commanding officer, Commander Lee (Charles McGraw).

programme.title: I Remember Mama
programme.desc: Norwegian immigrant Marta Hanson (Irene Dunne) keeps a firm but loving hand on her household of four children, a devoted husband (Philip Dorn) and a highly-educated lodger (Cedric Hardwicke) who reads Charles Dickens to the family every evening. Through financial crises, illnesses and the small triumphs of everyday life, Marta maintains her optimism and sense of humor, traits she passes on to her aspiring-author daughter, Katrin (Barbara Bel Geddes).

programme.title: 'Neath the Arizona Skies
programme.desc: A cowboy escorts a young half-Native American girl, whose mother has recently died. He must find her white father in order to claim her hefty inheritance.

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Watson's encounter with Lady Frances Carfax brings Holmes rushing to the Lake District.

programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.desc: Cowboys (Tim Holt, Ray Whitley) catch a rustler when he crosses the Mexican border.

programme.desc: In the 1870s, Marshal Wyatt Earp (Bruce Cowling) calls upon Dodge City, Kan., Sheriff Bat Masterson (George Montgomery) to track down rancher Merrick (John Maxwell), who is accused of murder. But, thanks to some proof held by local Native American Chief Yellow Hawk (Jay Silverheels), Masterson realizes Merrick might be the victim of a frame-up. So, with the help of Earp and gunfighter Doc Holliday (James Griffith), Masterson sets out to find those who are really behind the killing.

programme.title: Major Dundee
programme.desc: During the end of the Civil War, Major Dundee guards Confederate prisoners, Union deserters and ordinary hard-bitten criminals in a remote fort. When Apaches attack the fort and make off with three children, Dundee must set up a posse including Confederates, who face a choice between joining up or being shot. The feud between Dundee and Tyreen is also heated up by a sultry Mexican widow.

programme.title: The Train
programme.desc: With the Allies poised to retake Paris from the Germans, Col. Franz Von Waldheim (Paul Scofield) conspires to sneak out by train all the art masterpieces looted by the German army. But museum curator Mademoiselle Villard (Suzanne Flon) alerts the French Resistance, and soon railway inspector Paul Labiche (Burt Lancaster) is pressed into service. He reroutes the train, and cleverly deceives Von Waldheim by renaming each train station along the way.

programme.title: The Best Years of Our Lives
programme.desc: Fred, Al and Homer are three World War II veterans facing difficulties as they re-enter civilian life. Fred (Dana Andrews) is a war hero who, unable to compete with more highly skilled workers, has to return to his low-wage soda jerk job. Bank executive Al (Fredric March) gets into trouble for offering favorable loans to veterans. After losing both hands in the war, Homer (Harold Russell) returns to his loving fiancée, but must struggle to adjust.

programme.title: Home in San Antone
programme.desc: An innkeeper tries to keep his struggling establishment afloat, unaware that a famous country singer (Roy Acuff) and diamond thieves are staying under his roof.

programme.title: My Classic Car

programme.title: The Hanging Tree
programme.desc: Joseph "Doc" Frail (Gary Cooper) is a doctor with a past he's trying to outrun. While in Montana, he comes across a mining camp with a hanging tree and rescues a man named Rune (Ben Piazza) from the noose. With Rune as his servant, Frail decides to settle down, and he takes over as town doctor. He meets Elizabeth (Maria Schell), who is suffering from shock, and the two soon fall in love. But when Elizabeth is attacked, Frail's attempt to help her lands them both in trouble.

programme.title: Twelve O'Clock High
programme.desc: In 1942, an American Air Force unit stationed in England is plagued with morale problems until no-nonsense Brigadier General Frank Savage (Gregory Peck) assumes command. His tough leadership is initially resented by not only his pilots but his second-in-command (Hugh Marlowe), a West Point graduate and son of a general. But, with the help of a hotshot flying ace (Robert Patten) and a sympathetic administrator (Dean Jagger), the unit pulls together into a gung-ho fighting crew.

programme.title: The Battle at Apache Pass

programme.title: The Guns of Navarone
programme.desc: In 1943, a small commando team is sent to destroy huge German guns on the Greek Island of Navarone in order to rescue Allied troops trapped on Kheros. Led by British Major Franklin (Anthony Quayle), the team includes American Mallory (Gregory Peck), Greek resistance fighter Stavros (Anthony Quinn) and reluctant explosives expert Miller. Facing impossible odds, the men battle stormy seas and daunting cliffs. When Franklin is injured, Mallory takes command, and personal enmities spill over.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg: The China Show

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg Markets: Asia
programme.desc: In an effort to provide extensive coverage of worldwide financial markets, Bloomberg features several regional shows under the "Bloomberg Markets" banner, with each focusing on that geographic area's markets. This Asian-focused version is hosted by Shery Ahn, David Ingles, Haidi Lun, Rishaad Salamat and Angie Lau. They report the latest business news and speak with the likes of political and business leaders to help investors make investment decisions that build their portfolios.

programme.title: Live: Bloomberg Horizons Middle East & Africa
programme.desc: A spotlight on the fast paced growth and major deals happening across the Middle East and Africa; linking the market drivers from overnight in the US and across Asia into the region's trading day, with deep dive analysis of the top business, energy and geopolitical stories shaping the decisions of traders across the Middle East, Africa and beyond.

programme.title: Escape From Fort Bravo
programme.desc: Captain Roper is the warden of a Union-run prison camp. A mysterious woman named Carla Forester arrives at the camp in order to free the Confederate prisoners, and things get complicated when Carla and the captain begin to fall for each other. In an effort to recapture the escaped prisoners, Roper has a run-in with a group of Mescalero Indians. In order to survive, he has to team up with his own prisoners to fight the Indians and wipe them out.

programme.desc: After escaping a lynch mob, Gar Davis (Clint Walker) disguises himself in a dead man's clothes and stumbles upon a farm. When Comanches attack the homestead, Gar leaps into action to defend the hapless residents -- widow Celia Gray (Virginia Mayo) and her son Chad (Richard Eyer). A tenuous romance develops between Gar and Celia when he escorts the family to Fort Dobbs. But everything changes when Celia discovers the stranger is wearing her dead husband's jacket.

Frank Hewitt (Audie Murphy) deserts the army after Colorado Union soldiers carry out the deadly Sand Creek reservation massacre, fearing reprisal from the Cheyenne. Crossing into Texas, Franks finds himself labeled a traitor for fighting with the Yankees, and his warnings about possible Indian revenge go unheeded. When a town near a mission, populated mostly by women, is attacked, Frank is appointed their protector and must build an army comprised of townswomen.

programme.title: Yuma

Brothers Sam (Bruce Glover) and Rol King (Neil Russell) hold up a stagecoach, and Harmon slaps them in jail. When Rol is killed in an attempted escape, Harmon is wrongly accused of shooting him, and the Kings' big brother, Arch (Morgan Woodward), will not stand for it. Hoping to avoid another killing, Harmon vows to catch Rol's killer and put things to right.

programme.title: Fauda
programme.desc: After retiring from the Israel Defense Force, Doron is working on a vineyard. He gets roped back into active duty, though, upon learning that an enemy who Doron thought his unit had killed is still alive and plans on attending his brother's wedding. Doron rejoins the IDF team with the intention of going under cover at the wedding, but the plan goes awry when the team's cover is blown. That sets into motion a chaotic chain of events and leads IDF into a manhunt to locate and kill the squad's target.

programme.title: Bonanza
programme.desc: Ben Cartwright is the patriarch of an all-male Nevada ranching family. Set during and after the Civil War, "Bonanza" is the story of life on the family's thousand-acre spread, known as the Ponderosa, near Virginia City. Ben has his three sons, each with a different (and deceased) mother. Serious eldest son Adam hopes to follow in his father's footsteps; middle son Hoss is a gentle giant -- physically strong, but sometimes naive; and youngest son Little Joe is the most impetuous and romantic of the offspring.

programme.title: In Fisherman TV
programme.desc: Always in touch with the hottest tactics happening for each new season, the In-Fisherman staff captures the historic rise of the paddletail swimbait for walleyes; revealing one of the most fundamentally productive tactics ever developed for largemouth bass; highlighting proven tools of the trade for giant pike.

programme.title: Let's Fish TV

programme.desc: Brian Pigman Quaca is a true-blue original - an unapologetic, high-energy host with a larger-than-life personality that has redefined outdoor television. programme.title: It Came from Outer Space

programme.title: The Art of Racing in the Rain
programme.desc: (2019) Touching comedy-drama. An ageing golden retriever (voiced by Kevin Costner) tells the story of his life with his best pal, a budding Formula 1 driver.

programme.title: Gemini Man

programme.title: Bad Times at the El Royale
programme.desc: (2018) Crime thriller with Jeff Bridges, Jon Hamm, Dakota Johnson and Chris Hemsworth. Shifty strangers collide in a sleazy-but-glamorous deserted motel with its own buried secrets.

programme.desc: (2016) Ben Wheatley's action-thriller starring Armie Hammer, Brie Larson, Sharlto Copley, Cillian Murphy and Michael Smiley. An IRA arms deal in 1970s Boston goes disastrously wrong.

programme.title: Nothing Personal
programme.desc: (1995) Drama. In 1975 - during The Troubles - the lives of a disparate group of Belfast residents collide on one night as they move through the city, under the constant threat of violence.

programme.desc: Children are the future -- and so are their inventions, which is why "Think Big" could be an important show. It features young inventors competing to see who can come up with the most-innovative invention. programme.title: America's Heartland
programme.desc: Much of the food Americans eat is produced by farmers and ranchers in the country's heartland, but many people don't know how it's produced. "America's Heartland" provides information about the people and processes responsible for the availability of food and fuel across the country and around the world. The show's reporters and producers tell stories in topics that include farm families, consumer issues, animal welfare and crop sustainability. Some episodes also take the show abroad to countries such as Egypt and Taiwan to show the impact American agriculture has on the global economy.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp reluctantly agrees to hire his younger brother Morgan as a deputy when Morgan stops a robbery; Morgan assists Deputy Hal when Curly Bill Brocius holds up a stagecoach.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp must keep an eye on two fugitives hiding out at Lucy King's home as he awaits a warrant, but when the public catches on, he has no choice but to risk a confrontation.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp arrests Doc Holliday and and Skunky Groggin for robbing a Wells Fargo stagecoach, but Doc denies involvement and concocts a plan with Earp to rob another stage to find the truth.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Lt. Clark alerts Earp about a renegade group of Pawnee who attacked him over a shipment of fake guns and becomes their captive when attempting negotiations; Earp must track down the guns' seller before the Pawnee execute Clark.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Reb Thomas stands accused of murdering Dan Bolton, although Earp suspects a frame-up, and despite Judge Tobin's efforts to hold a swift trial, one juror refuses to agree with the others.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp and Doc apprehend a girl called Little Pistol, who helped a gang rob Doc's saloon; Doc uses tooth surgery as a pretense to coax the girl into giving up her true identity and the gang's location while Earp fears for her future.

programme.title: Fight to Survive
programme.desc: Faith, determination and human spirit abound in "Fight to Survive," which recounts stories of people's deadly ordeals in the outdoors. Accompanying survivors back to where harrowing events occurred is rock-climber Craig DeMartino, who is featured in the premiere episode. His right leg was amputated below the knee after a climbing accident, but he continued in his sport. In 2006 he became the first amputee to free-climb the 3,200-foot face of Yosemite's El Capitan in one day. Other stories tell of sailor Steven Callahan clinging to a raft in the Atlantic, fisherman Dean Ririe trapped by a boulder in icy water, and hiker Ted Porter falling into a glacial crevasse.

programme.title: Fight to Survive
programme.desc: Faith, determination and human spirit abound in "Fight to Survive," which recounts stories of people's deadly ordeals in the outdoors. Accompanying survivors back to where harrowing events occurred is rock-climber Craig DeMartino, who is featured in the premiere episode. His right leg was amputated below the knee after a climbing accident, but he continued in his sport. In 2006 he became the first amputee to free-climb the 3,200-foot face of Yosemite's El Capitan in one day. Other stories tell of sailor Steven Callahan clinging to a raft in the Atlantic, fisherman Dean Ririe trapped by a boulder in icy water, and hiker Ted Porter falling into a glacial crevasse.

programme.desc: An MMA fighter accuses a sponsor of giving him a supplement contaminated with steroids, resulting in a suspension that ruined his career.

programme.title: Justice With Judge Mablean
programme.desc: Judge Mablean Ephriam, who presided over "Divorce Court" from 1999-2006 as the first star of the revived version of the show, returns to the courtroom genre with his half-hour series that deals with life and the law. The former Los Angeles-based prosecutor takes on the typical cases that are found on TV court shows. The arbitrator says that her show "will be life because everything we do, it involves the law."

programme.title: America's Court With Judge Ross
programme.desc: Americans just can't get enough courtroom drama, as is evidenced by yet another addition into the daytime court show fray. Former judge and prosecutor Kevin Ross adjudicates real cases, in which he tries to show litigants alternative ways of dealing with their issues and make them consider the consequences of their actions.

programme.title: America's Court With Judge Ross

programme.title: The Verdict With Judge Hatchett
programme.desc: Judge Glenda Hatchett presided over her self-titled courtroom show from 2000-2008. Now, she returns to the medium with "The Verdict." In the half-hour series, the Atlanta native and former juvenile-court judge rules over small-claims cases brought in front of her. Hatchett's courtroom isn't just about finding someone guilty, though. She also uses her experience dealing with young people and her tough-love approach to help send a message to out-of-control juvenile offenders to try to set them on the right track in their lives.

programme.title: Supreme Justice With Judge Karen
programme.desc: Karen Mills was a county court judge and criminal defense attorney in Florida before turning to TV to preside over her own court shows. In her third effort, "Supreme Justice," Mills tackles small-claims court arbitrations in her compassionate way, to which viewers of her previous shows have grown accustomed. As usual with court shows, cases involve friendships gone bad, money disputes and property damage.

programme.title: Venom
programme.desc: (2018) Dark sci-fi action with Tom Hardy as Marvel's anti-hero. A reporter becomes inhabited by a ferocious extraterrestrial that has its own ideas on how to behave. With Michelle Williams.

programme.title: Venom: Let There Be Carnage
programme.desc: (2021) Tom Hardy returns as Marvel's gnarly antihero, to face a symbiote spawned from Venom's own blood living inside a serial killer who looks just like Woody Harrelson. Yikes.

programme.title: Millie Lies Low
programme.desc: (2021) Comedy drama with Ana Scotney. A woman leaves her native New Zealand to take up an internship at a top New York architecture firm. Except she doesn't, but then pretends she still is.

programme.title: The Comancheros
programme.desc: (1961) Western starring John Wayne and Stuart Whitman. A Texas Ranger is forced to team up with his prisoner while he's on a covert mission to take on a band of thieves and gunrunners.

programme.title: Fixed Bayonets!
programme.desc: (1951) Korean War drama about the stress of battle. Officers drop one by one as a US platoon fights a rearguard action, leaving one unwilling man (Richard Basehart) to take their place.

programme.title: Written on the Wind
programme.desc: (1956) Lauren Bacall, Robert Stack, Rock Hudson and Dorothy Malone star in Douglas Sirk's drama, a commentary on 1950s life that's disguised as a lurid, glossy soap opera.

programme.title: Criss Cross
programme.desc: (1949) Muscular film noir starring Burt Lancaster and Yvonne De Carlo. A dame convinces her ex-husband, an armoured car guard, to come in on a daring robbery.

programme.title: Fisherman's Friends
programme.desc: (2019) Heart-warming comedy based on the real-life story of the Cornish folk ensemble who took the UK charts by storm in 2010, starring Daniel Mays and Tuppence Middleton.

programme.title: Angel Has Fallen
programme.desc: (2019) Action-thriller sequel. Secret agent Mike Banning (Gerard Butler) goes on the run when he's framed for an attack on the President. Can he clear his name?

programme.title: Three Thousand Years of Longing
programme.desc: (2022) Fantasy with Idris Elba and Tilda Swinton. A djinn offers a storytelling expert three wishes. But she knows to look this gift horse in the mouth and so makes it all about him.

programme.title: Omen
programme.desc: (2023) Rapper Baloji's debut is a mythic familial psychodrama about a young man who takes his pregnant fiancée to the Democratic Republic of Congo to meet the parents. In French/subs.

programme.title: The Shepherd of the Hills
programme.desc: (1941) John Wayne stars as a moody young man living in the Ozarks who has sworn revenge on his long-absent father for deserting his mother. Then a kindly stranger appears on the scene...

programme.title: Free Fire Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Ben Wheatley and stars Armie Hammer, Cillian Murphy, Sharlto Copley and Michael Smiley talk about making the new crime thriller, featuring wall-to-wall gunplay.

programme.title: The Mouse That Roared
programme.desc: (1959) Satirical comedy starring Peter Sellers (in a triple role) and the original Doctor Who, William Hartnell. The world's smallest state, Grand Fenwick, declares war on the USA.

programme.title: Guadalcanal Diary
programme.desc: (1943) Second World War action drama following two US marines as they make advances into the Solomon Islands to drive back the Japanese forces. Starring Preston Foster and Lloyd Nolan.

programme.title: The 300 Spartans
programme.desc: (1962) Historical drama with Ralph Richardson. A retelling of the stand made at Thermopylae in 480 BC by a small Greek force against invasion by an enormous Persian army.

programme.title: Flight of the Phoenix
programme.desc: (2004) Drama. Dennis Quaid stars as the captain of an aircraft downed in the desert. There's no hope of rescue, unless he and the survivors can build another plane from the wreckage.

programme.title: The Woman King
programme.desc: (2022) Historical action starring Viola Davis. An elite force of female warriors, the Agojie, protect their West African kingdom from threats from rival kingdoms and from European slavers.

programme.title: Miss Peregrine's Home for...
programme.desc: ...Peculiar Children: (2016) Eva Green and Asa Butterfield star in Tim Burton's fantasy adventure. A teenager enters the magical, but scary and violent, world where his grandfather grew up.

programme.title: The Neon Bible
programme.desc: (1994) The moving story of a boy (Jacob Tierney) whose life in America's bible belt in the 1940s is profoundly affected by his parents' domestic violence.
